blocks.text: Syric Nightbloom
Codex of Embodied Arcana
Core Pages 1-10 Mechanics - Uniform Revision

rows: Format: Each page uses the same structure: feature title, trigger/use/cost, mechanical effect, scaling, limits, and examples where the original draft included them.
Source Handling: Pages 2 and 5-10 were reformatted from the v7 mechanics draft. Pages 1, 3, and 4 were restored from the Codex master page materials and reduced to mechanics-facing entries.

pages.title: The First Rune
pages.subtitle: Awakening the Arcane Flesh

blocks.text: Core Feature: First Rune Binding

rows: Choose a 1st-level spell to inscribe as Syric's first rune.
The rune replaces non-costly material components. Verbal and somatic components remain required.
Make an Arcana check. DC = 10 + spell level.
The rune permanently links to Syric's essence.
The rune becomes Dormant for 24 hours.
Syric may attempt to cast from a Dormant rune before the 24 hours end.
Syric takes 1d6 force damage per spell level and the spell fails.
The spell resolves normally, but the rune remains Dormant until the 24 hours end.

pages.title: Inscription
pages.subtitle: Binding Magic to Flesh

blocks.text: Inscribing a Rune

rows: The spell is successfully inscribed as a rune.
The rune is unstable and cannot be used until Syric completes a long rest.

blocks.text: Arcane Charge

rows: Each time Syric casts a leveled spell, he gains Arcane Charge equal to the spell's level.
Arcane Charge is tracked as a running total.
Arcane Charge resets to 0 when it naturally dissipates, is fully vented, or Arcane Discharge occurs.

blocks.text: Overload - Arcane Threshold
Overload occurs when Syric channels more arcane energy than his runes and body can safely contain.

rows: Syric enters Overload when the total spell levels he casts in a single round equals or exceeds his Arcana Bonus. Shadow-cast spells using Syric’s slots count toward this total.
If Syric casts another leveled spell while in the Overload State, he must immediately make an Overload check.

blocks.text: Overload Outcomes

rows: Syric maintains control. He remains in the Overload State.

blocks.text: Arcane Discharge

rows: Arcane Discharge occurs when Syric fails an Overload check.
2 x spell levels stored force damage.
Syric automatically fails this saving throw and takes full damage.
Arcane Charge resets to 0 after Arcane Discharge.

blocks.text: Arcane Venting
Syric can actively vent stored arcane energy to reduce the risk of Overload.

rows: Syric rolls his Vent Die and reduces Arcane Charge by the result.
Arcane Charge cannot be reduced below 0.

blocks.text: Vent Die Scaling
Charge Dissipation

rows: Syric automatically dissipates charges equal to half his Vent Die result at the end of his turn. If Syric does not cast a leveled spell during a round, his Arcane Charge discharges at max Vent Die value.
When Arcane Charge resets to 0, the Charge State ends.

blocks.text: Quick Reference

rows: Syric gains Charge equal to spell level when casting leveled spells.
Syric becomes unstable but does not roll yet.
Arcane Discharge + Arcane Rebound: roll on Arcane Rebound d100 table.

pages.title: Mastery
pages.subtitle: Deepening the Connection

blocks.text: Core Feature: Free Upcast

rows:
Once per long rest, Syric may cast a spell using a spell slot one level higher than the slot he actually spends. The spell cannot be upcast beyond a spell level Syric is normally able to cast.

At levels 11–16, Syric may use this feature twice per long rest.

At levels 17–20, each use may upcast the spell by up to 2 levels instead of 1.
Syric can upcast any spell by one level for free.
The upcast spell level cannot exceed a spell level for which Syric has slots.
Syric must have a spell slot available at the spell's original non-upcast level.
The original non-upcast spell slot is consumed normally.
Syric can cast Fireball at 4th level even if all 4th-level spell slots are used, but he must have a 3rd-level slot available, and that 3rd-level slot is consumed.

pages.title: Runic Flow
pages.subtitle: Magic as Natural Extension

blocks.text: Core Feature: Seamless Casting

rows:
Once per long rest, Syric may cast one inscribed rune spell without verbal or somatic components. The spell otherwise resolves normally.

At levels 11–16, Syric may use this feature twice per long rest. 

At levels 17–20, Syric may use this feature three times per long rest.
Syric may cast one spell from one of his inscribed runes without verbal or somatic components.
Material components remain normal per table rules.
This seamless cast does not count as a strain effect. It does not change spell slot economy.
It can share a round with other actions so long as Syric obeys all normal limits.

pages.title: The Price of Power
pages.subtitle: Codex Surge

rows: Design Goal: Page 5 is Syric's damage engine. It converts successful rune spell damage into extra damage after the spell connects, giving Syric a wizard-shaped answer to smite-style burst without using weapon attacks.

blocks.text: 5A. Codex Dice
Feature: Syric gains a pool of Codex Dice. Each Codex Die is a d6; d10 with bonus action. Codex Dice regain after a long rest.

rows: When Syric casts an inscribed rune spell and the spell damages one or more creatures, he may spend Codex Dice to add extra damage to damaged creatures. Syric decides after he knows the spell damaged the target.
No action, bonus action, or reaction required for d6. Limited to proficiency bonus dice per use.
If Syric uses his Bonus Action instead, the damage dice become d10s and there is no limit on Codex Dice used.
The Codex Dice deal the same damage type as the spell. If the spell deals more than one damage type, Syric chooses one of those spell damage types for the Codex Dice.
Codex Surge does not work with scrolls.
Syric casts Fireball at level 13. Syric spends 5 Codex Dice. Each enemy who failed the DC takes 5d6 additional damage, half as much on a success (as per the spell rules).

blocks.text: 5B. Echoed Rune

rows: After Syric casts an inscribed rune spell that damages at least one creature, he may echo part of the spell into damaged creatures.
Bonus action, used after the original spell resolves.

Spend one spell slot that is half the level of the original spell slot used, rounded down, or higher, minimum 1st-level slot, and Codex Dice equal to the original spell's level.  If the original spell was a cantrip, Syric may use Echoed Rune without spending a spell slot.
Once per long rest. Two times at level 15. Three times at level 17.
Echoed Rune requires no new saving throw and no new attack roll.

If the original spell damaged the target(s), the echo automatically damages that same target(s). Echo damage equals half the original spell's rolled damage dice, rounded down. The echo uses the original spell's damage type.
Syric casts a spell that deals 40 damage and pushes the target 10 feet. Echoed Rune deals 20 damage.

pages.title: Duality of Runes
pages.subtitle: Veil and Reveal

blocks.text: Core Feature: Veil and Reveal

rows: Syric can hide or reveal his runes.
Syric takes 1d12 psychic damage each time he hides his runes.
Hidden runes last for 4 hours, then reveal again unless Syric chooses to hide them longer.
If Syric keeps them hidden longer, he takes 1d12 psychic damage again per 4 hours.
Half proficiency bonus rounded down per long rest.

blocks.text: Veiled Runes

rows: Runes do not visibly glow. Normal sight cannot see active rune flare from casting of magic.
Detect Magic senses magic on Syric but does not show exact rune locations.
Truesight, True Seeing, or similar features can still see the runes.
Syric gains +10 to Stealth checks as if under Pass Without Trace spell.
Syric gains advantage on his first spell attack roll, or he may impose disadvantage on the first saving throw against one of his spell save DCs.

blocks.text: Revealed Runes

rows: Syric may intentionally reveal his hidden runes, causing them to visibly glow.
When he does, he may use his Arcana skill as the modifier for one Intimidation or Persuasion check as the sudden display unsettles, impresses, or unnerves the target.

pages.title: Runic Resurgence
pages.subtitle: Deathline Reflex and Spell Recall

blocks.text: 7A. Deathline Reflex

rows: When Syric drops below half HP from damage, he may trigger one defensive reflex.
Once per long rest. Two times at level 15. Three times at level 17.
Reaction. Roll 1d4 to see what the effect is.
Gain temporary HP equal to 2d6 + Syric's wizard level.
Teleport up to 60 feet to a space Syric can see. Gain resistance to all damage types until the start of Syric's next turn.
Gain resistance to the triggering damage type for 1 minute, including the triggering damage.
Gains advantage on the saving throw against the triggering effect type for one minute. e.g. Spells, Piercing, Radiant, Etc.

blocks.text: 7B. Spell Recall

rows: Syric can recall a known spell that he did not prepare for the day.

Once per long rest. At level 15, Syric may use this feature twice per long rest. At level 17 or higher, Syric may use this feature three times per long rest. But the spell does NOT become a prepared spell.
No action required to recall the spell. The recalled spell is cast using its normal casting time.
The spell must be a spell Syric knows, must be a spell level Syric can cast, must not have been prepared for that day, and Syric must have an available spell slot of that spell's level or higher.
The spell slot is spent normally. Syric takes psychic damage equal to the spell level as his mind strains to pull the unprepared spell into place.
The recalled spell does not become prepared after casting.
Syric knows Forcecage, but did not prepare it. He uses Spell Recall to cast it anyway, spends the 7th-level slot, and takes 7 psychic damage.

pages.title: Advanced Inscription
pages.subtitle: Hybrid Sigils and Resonance Shaping

blocks.text: 8A. Resonance Points

rows: Syric gains Resonance Points equal to half his wizard level rounded up + his Intelligence modifier. Minimum 1.
Syric regains all Resonance Points after a long rest.
At wizard level 13 with Intelligence 20, Syric has 12 Resonance Points.
Syric may apply only one Resonance Technique to a spell unless a feature says otherwise. Codex Surge is not a Resonance Technique and can be added to the same spell.

blocks.text: 8B. Resonance Techniques

rows: Spell level in Resonance Points; cantrip costs 1.
When Syric casts a spell that targets only one creature and does not have a range of self, he may target a second creature in range with the same spell.
Spell level in Resonance Points; cantrip costs 1.

When Syric casts an area spell, choose creatures Syric can see in the area up to proficiency bonus. Chosen creatures automatically succeed and take no damage if the spell normally deals half damage on a successful save.
Spell level in Resonance Points; cantrip costs 1.
When Syric casts a spell, he may change the damage type to another damage type approved by the DM for the spell pattern.
When Syric rolls damage for a spell, he may reroll damage dice up to his Intelligence modifier. He must use the new rolls.
Spell level in Resonance Points; cantrip costs 1.
Remove verbal and somatic components from one rune spell. Costly or consumed material components remain required.

blocks.text: 8C. Hybrid Sigil

rows: Syric can fuse two magical patterns into a single advanced effect.
When Syric casts a spell using a spell slot, he may activate Hybrid Sigil and choose one Hybrid Variant below. Runic Interference is the exception and uses its own reaction trigger.
Once per long rest. At level 15, Syric may use Hybrid Sigil twice per long rest. At level 18, Syric may use Hybrid Sigil three times per long rest.

DC = 10 + the spell slot level used. On success, the Hybrid Variant works. On failure, the spell still casts normally but the Hybrid Variant fails. Failure by 5 or more also deals force damage to Syric equal to 2 x the spell slot level used.

Choose one ally Syric can see within 30 feet, or 60 feet at level 17. The ally gains +2 AC for 1d4 rounds, temporary HP equal to 2d6 + Syric's wizard level, and advantage on one d20 roll made within the next minute.
After the spell resolves, Syric teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space he can see, or one willing ally Syric can see within 60 feet moves up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.
When a visible creature within 150 feet takes an action. Roll 1d6.

blocks.text: Runic Interference Options (REACTION)

rows: The creature cannot take reactions until the start of Syric's next turn.
Creatures take 3d8 + spell save DC force damage if they move prior to the start of Syric's next turn.
All enemy's next d20 roll is made with disadvantage.
Syric and all allies gain advantage on the next d20 roll.
Enemy's movement speed is reduced to 0 until the start of Syric's next turn.
Enemy suffers from lethargy until the start of Syric's next turn. It can only take 1 Action, a Bonus Action, or use movement.

blocks.text: Spell Fusion

rows: When Syric casts an inscribed rune spell, he may fuse a second inscribed spell into the casting.
This can be added after the outcome is known of the spell save or attack roll.
Resonance Points equal to the combined levels of both spells.
The first spell is the primary spell and resolves normally. It must be the higher-level of the two spells.
The second spell is the secondary spell and contributes one chosen property only from the spell's list of effects. Must be two different spells.
The primary spell controls the spell's range, targets, attack roll, saving throw, and affected area.
The secondary spell does not create its own separate targeting, area, save, attack roll, or full spell effect.
A creature or object must be affected by the primary spell to be affected by the secondary property.

blocks.text: Secondary Property Options

rows: Add the secondary spell's damage dice.
Add one AC, resistance, or temporary HP effect.
Add one movement, teleport, or forced movement effect.
Add one condition from the secondary spell.
Add one non-damaging utility effect, such as invisibility or silence.

blocks.text: Hard Limits
No second saving throw.
No second attack roll.
No extra targets.
No extra area.
No summons.
No created objects.
No ongoing spell zones unless the primary spell already creates one.
Examples

rows: Syric casts Misty Step as the primary spell and fuses Invisibility as the secondary spell. He spends 4 Resonance Points total. Syric teleports normally, then gains invisibility as the chosen secondary property. Result: Syric teleports and vanishes.

Syric casts Fireball as the primary spell and fuses Lightning Bolt as the secondary spell. He spends 6 Resonance Points total. Fireball controls the area, targets, and Dexterity saving throw. Any creature damaged by Fireball also takes Lightning Bolt damage as the chosen secondary property. Lightning Bolt does not create a line, does not use its own range, and does not force a second saving throw. Result: The Fireball explodes, and lightning detonates through every creature damaged by the blast.

The first spell is the primary spell and resolves normally. It must be the higher-level of the two spells.
Secondary Spell
The second spell is the secondary spell and contributes one chosen property only from the spell's list of effects. Must be two different spells.
Control
The primary spell controls the spell's range, targets, attack roll, saving throw, and affected area.
No Separate Effect
The secondary spell does not create its own separate targeting, area, save, attack roll, or full spell effect.
Affected Requirement
A creature or object must be affected by the primary spell to be affected by the secondary property.
Secondary Property Options
Property
Effect
Damage
Add the secondary spell's damage dice.
Defense
Add one AC, resistance, or temporary HP effect.
Movement
Add one movement, teleport, or forced movement effect.
Condition
Add one condition from the secondary spell.
Utility
Add one non-damaging utility effect, such as invisibility or silence.
Hard Limits
No second saving throw.
No second attack roll.
No extra targets.
No extra area.
No summons.
No created objects.
No ongoing spell zones unless the primary spell already creates one.
Examples
Rule
Mechanic
Misty Step + Invisibility
Syric casts Misty Step as the primary spell and fuses Invisibility as the secondary spell. He spends 4 Resonance Points total. Syric teleports normally, then gains invisibility as the chosen secondary property. Result: Syric teleports and vanishes.
Fireball + Lightning Bolt
Syric casts Fireball as the primary spell and fuses Lightning Bolt as the secondary spell. He spends 6 Resonance Points total. Fireball controls the area, targets, and Dexterity saving throw. Any creature damaged by Fireball also takes Lightning Bolt damage as the chosen secondary property. Lightning Bolt does not create a line, does not use its own range, and does not force a second saving throw. Result: The Fireball explodes, and lightning detonates through every creature damaged by the blast.

pages.title: Arcane Devouring
pages.subtitle: Embodied Relic

rows: Design Goal: Syric does not merely learn spells or wield artifacts. He learns how to be artifacts. Arcane Devouring lets him permanently consume a magic item, destroy the object, and internalize its arcane properties as Living Rune functions.

blocks.text: 9A. Devouring Ritual

rows: Syric must be level 15 or higher, or must have unlocked this page through the Codex.
Syric must physically possess the magic item for the full ritual. If the item requires attunement, Syric must be attuned to it before the ritual begins.

At the end of the ritual, the original item is destroyed. It cannot be repaired, restored, reforged, or made whole again by mundane crafting, Mending, Fabricate, Wish, or similar magic unless the DM creates a specific story exception.
On success, Syric absorbs all magical properties of the item. Those properties become part of his Living Runes and can be used without holding, wearing, or attuning to the destroyed object.
Absorbed powers keep the original item's activation type, action cost, charges, recharge timing, use limits, and restrictions unless the DM modifies them.
Use the better of the item's listed DC/attack bonus or Syric's spell save DC/spell attack bonus.
If the item was a wand, staff, rod, ring, cloak, robe, weapon, shield, or focus, Syric's body and runes replace the item as the point of use.

blocks.text: 9B. Internal Attunement Slots

rows: Syric gains 1 additional internal attunement slot.
Syric has a number of Internal Attunement Slots equal to RAW attunement slots, +1. These do not add to external slots.
If the devoured item required attunement, its powers occupy 1 Internal Attunement Slot.
The destroyed object no longer requires attunement because the object no longer exists. The limit represents how many major item-patterns Syric can safely carry inside himself.

A no-attunement magic item does not occupy an Internal Attunement Slot. Syric may devour any number of no-attunement magic items, but normal D&D stacking rules still apply. Identical bonuses do not stack unless the original items would have stacked.
Consumables such as potions, spell scrolls, and single-use charms are not valid targets unless the DM allows the item to teach Syric a one-use pattern rather than a permanent property.

blocks.text: 9C. Arcane Devouring Check
At the end of the ritual, Syric makes an Arcana check. The item is destroyed whether the check succeeds or fails.

rows: +2 DC if the item is sentient.
+2 DC if the item is cursed.
+3 DC if the item has charges.
Advantage if Syric has been attuned to the item for at least 7 days.
Advantage if Syric used the item in a major combat, ritual, or story moment.
Disadvantage if Syric has never used the item.
The item is destroyed and Syric gains all magical properties. If it required attunement, it occupies 1 Internal Attunement Slot.
The item is destroyed and Syric gains no benefit. Syric takes force or psychic damage equal to 5 x rarity tier: common 5, uncommon 10, rare 15, very rare 20, legendary 25, artifact 30.
As failure, and Syric gains 1 level of exhaustion. One currently devoured item property becomes unavailable until the next long rest.
As failure, and one devoured no-attunement property of Syric's choice is permanently expelled. If he has none, one Internal Attunement Slot power becomes unavailable for 7 days.

blocks.text: 9D. Cast to the Nether

rows: Syric releases one absorbed relic pattern from his Living Runes.
The power is permanently lost. The original item does not reform. Its arcane pattern disperses into the Nether, Void, Rift, or other campaign-specific beyond.
If the released power occupied an Internal Attunement Slot, that slot becomes available at the end of the ritual.
Syric takes force damage equal to 10 x the item's rarity tier and the effect encompasses a 20-foot radius. No save DC and no immunities negate or minimize the effects.
Once released, the same exact item's power cannot be recovered unless Syric finds another item with the same property and successfully devours it.

blocks.text: 9E. Examples

rows: Syric devours the staff. The staff is destroyed, occupies 1 Internal Attunement Slot, and its spells, charges, spell absorption, and other properties become internal rune functions. Its powers use the staff's normal charge rules unless the DM modifies them.

Syric devours the ring. The ring is destroyed and its dual concentration property becomes internal. If the ring required attunement, it occupies 1 Internal Attunement Slot. If Syric releases it later, the ability is gone and the ring does not return.

pages.title: Ascension
pages.subtitle: Ascendant Rune Casting

blocks.text: Core Feature: Ascendant Rune Casting

rows: Syric's rune magic becomes naturally silent and hidden. He can reveal the runes but no longer strains to keep them hidden.

When Syric casts an inscribed rune spell, it does not require verbal components, somatic components, or non-costly material components. A component is non-costly only if it has no listed gold value and is not consumed. Costly or consumed components are still required, based on table rules.

Syric's runes are muted by default. They can only be seen by Truesight, True Seeing, or a feature that specifically reveals hidden magical markings. They can be sensed by Detect Magic with a successful Arcana check against Syric's spell save DC.
Syric no longer gains exhaustion from normal Codex spellcasting strain. He can still gain exhaustion from Hybrid Sigil, Overload, failed rituals, and normal adventuring effects.
Syric has disadvantage on Charisma checks with common mundane NPCs if his deathless or rune nature is obvious. No penalty applies if his runes are muted and he appears normal.
Pick three spells of level 3 or lower to be treated as cantrips for the day. Or, pick one 4th- to 7th-level spell to be treated as a cantrip for the day.

blocks.text: Codex Die Upgrade

rows: Any 1 or 2 rolled on Codex Dice become 3s.
One damage die per spell level is maximized. Example: if Syric casts a 5th-level spell and uses 10 Codex Dice, 5 of the dice are automatically 8s. The remainder are rolled normally.

blocks.text: Core Page Quick Reference

rows: Bind the first rune; dormant failures can be forced at risk.
Spend 1 hour per spell level; Overload punishes compressed overcasting.
Once per long rest, upcast one spell by one level for free.
Once per long rest, cast one inscribed rune spell without verbal/somatic components.
Spend Codex Dice to add damage after a rune spell damages a target; use Echoed Rune to bonus-action repeat damage and proportional movement.
Hide or reveal rune glow; once per short rest gain advantage on a rune-related social or Arcana check.
Defensive reaction below half HP and limited casting of known but unprepared spells.
Use Resonance Points for Codex metamagic and Hybrid Sigils for tactical spell fusion.
Destroy magic items and internalize their arcane properties as Living Rune functions.
Internalized casting, muted runes, component reduction, and reduced strain.

blocks.text: Action Economy Snapshot

rows: Spell Recall to recall the spell. Recalled spell still uses normal casting time.
Veil/Reveal runes. Codex Surge d6 option.
Arcane Venting. Codex Surge d10 option. Echoed Rune / High Rune Echo.
Deathline Reflex. Runic Interference. Some Shadow-linked effects may also use reaction outside this Codex document.
Rune inscription, Arcane Devouring, and Cast to the Nether.

pages.title: The First Rune
pages.subtitle: Awakening the Arcane Flesh

blocks.text: Set this page down if you came to be crowned. I do not crown appetite. I weigh it.

The first rune is not permission to command the world. It is the first place where the world is permitted to answer you. Skin is honest in ways the tongue is not. It flinches, warms, scars, remembers. Let the first mark be made where your pulse cannot deny it heard the bargain.

Choose no spell for splendor. Choose the truth you are willing to carry when no witness praises you. A veil confesses that safety may require another face. A ward confesses that fear may be disciplined into shelter. Flame confesses the wish to end what stands before you. I do not despise a hard truth. I despise a false one dressed in devotion.

Do not borrow a vow. Do not bleed for a promise another mouth made. The rune will not ask whose house taught you, whose god claimed you, whose master praised your hand. It will ask only this: did you know the cost before the line was closed?
If the ink refuses, let it refuse. If the flesh rejects, let the hour die. A failed beginning can be buried cleanly. A forced beginning grows teeth.
Carry this first truth beneath the skin: magic does not become yours because you marked the body. It becomes answerable because the body can no longer pretend it was elsewhere.

pages.title: Inscription
pages.subtitle: Binding Magic to Flesh

blocks.text: Time is the first currency. Spend it awake.

The hour is not delay. The hour is the price becoming visible. Each stroke teaches the spell where it must live: nerve, blood, breath, memory, hunger, restraint. Rush one chamber, and the house will learn to burn from within.

Paint may flatter the skin. Pain may flatter the pride. Neither is binding. Binding begins when the mark is no longer an image but a resident, when the spell stops lying upon the body and begins answering from it.

Let another hand guide the needle if your own cannot. Let another breath steady yours if terror breaks the rhythm. But let no one else own the vow. Borrowed patience becomes debt. Borrowed courage becomes theater. Borrowed consequence becomes theft.

In the last minute of every hour, be still. Do not correct the line. Do not bargain with the ache. Do not promise what the previous fifty-nine minutes failed to prove. Stillness is the small court where haste is judged.
Know your runes as chambers of one living house. Force too much through them, and every door will open together. You will call it power until your own body becomes the doorway it escapes through.
Carry this second truth: what is inscribed upon flesh must also be inscribed upon patience, or the mark is only vanity wearing a wound.

pages.title: Mastery
pages.subtitle: Deepening the Connection

blocks.text: Power that must announce itself is still afraid of being ignored.

You ask to deepen the rune. First deepen the silence around it. Refinement is not more brilliance. It is less waste. It is the trimming of every flourish that does not carry consequence. It is the hand learning not to tremble simply because it is watched.

Duel the mirror that agrees with you. It will praise every habit that makes you grand and every excess that makes you feel chosen. Break those habits without applause. A spell that answers your vanity will betray your purpose the moment purpose grows quiet.

Do not mistake growth for hunger made respectable. Hunger always wants another level, another blaze, another witness, another proof that the last proof was not enough. Mastery asks a colder question: can the same breath bear greater weight without becoming louder?
When a rune settles so deeply that you stop admiring it, then it may be strengthened. When the mark feels less like treasure and more like bone, then it has begun to belong.
Carry this third truth: a greater working is not always more magic. Sometimes it is the same magic with the foolish parts removed.

pages.title: Runic Flow
pages.subtitle: Magic as Natural Extension

blocks.text: Intent that needs no herald is the gentlest weight the world will ever feel.

A word is not sin. A gesture is not shame. But every sound that does not carry the work is debt. Every motion thrown where stillness would have served is tax. Learn the difference before you call silence mastery.

There is a point finer than breath where will and world agree on what should happen. You will miss it if you love the display. You will miss it if you cast to be seen casting. Seek the point where the act is already finished inside you, then let the world discover it has agreed.

Do not confuse secrecy with grace. I do not ask you to be unseen. I ask you to be unwasteful. A hidden hand can still be vain. A quiet spell can still be theft. Let the measure be what remains after the working, not how little anyone noticed while you did it.
When power can pass through a crowded room and no cup trembles, you have not become small. You have become precise.
Carry this fourth truth: the cleanest spell is not the one without sign. It is the one without ornament.

pages.title: The Price Of Power
pages.subtitle: Codex Surge

blocks.text: Certainty is a blade. Hold it by the hilt, not the edge.

There will come an hour when chance offends you, when the trembling of a spell feels like insult, when failure would tilt more than pride. In that hour you may purchase certainty. But do not dare call the purchase free because the coin is hidden beneath your skin.

Pain is not proof of righteousness. Blood is not virtue. Suffering is not a crown. These are only payments, and payments may be wise or foolish. Spend only when the outcome is worth the wound, and name the wound before it opens.
Those who buy power to decorate themselves become ledgers full of unpaid ink. Those who buy power to restore what would otherwise break may stand before me without lowering their eyes.
Do not become addicted to the clean answer. A perfect strike can still serve a crooked hunger. A failed spell can still reveal the honest path. The scale does not love success. It loves balance.
Carry this fifth truth: when you demand certainty from the Codex, the Codex demands a receipt from you.

pages.title: Duality Of Runes
pages.subtitle: Veil and Reveal

blocks.text: A hidden truth is still truth. A hidden theft is still theft.

You will learn to dim the mark and think yourself subtle. Good. Then you will learn why subtlety is not innocence. The veil is lawful when it shelters what would be destroyed by careless light. The veil is corruption when it hides what should be returned.

Do not let bright souls teach you that every secret is a sin. Do not let dark souls teach you that every secret is wisdom. A secret is a vessel. Weigh what it carries. If it carries safety, keep it. If it carries shame that belongs to another, open it. If it carries your vanity, break it before it breeds.
Reveal without cruelty. Conceal without theft. Speak truth when truth restores. Keep silence when speech would only feed the speaker. The tongue, too, is a rune. It casts more often than the hand.
Your marks may glow, fade, hide, flare, or sleep. None of these states proves honesty. Only the consequence proves honesty.
Carry this sixth truth: shadow is not the enemy of light. Shadow is the shape light leaves when it meets something real.

Carry this sixth truth: shadow is not the enemy of light. Shadow is the shape light leaves when it meets something real.
◆  ◇  ◆

pages.title: Runic Resurgence
pages.subtitle: The Deathline Remembered

blocks.text: The line between surrender and survival is thin enough to write upon.

When breath grows shallow and the body begins to release its claim, the rune will ask what you have truly learned. Not what you memorized. Not what you boasted. What you became when comfort fell away.

Do not cling to life as a miser clings to coin. Clinging tilts the scale. But do not yield what has not yet paid its portion. Survival may be vanity, or it may be duty. The difference is the ledger that waits beyond the next breath.

A spell once spent is not always gone. Some workings leave a warm hollow where purpose still lives. Reach only for the one that restores the balance before you. Reach for any other, and you are stealing from your own ending.

If you rise from the deathline, rise quieter. Those who return loudly have learned only that they fear the dark. Those who return correctly bring something back for the world, not merely more of themselves.
Carry this seventh truth: the edge of death is not a throne. It is a narrow desk where unfinished debts are counted.

pages.title: Advanced Inscription
pages.subtitle: Harmony of Living Sigils

blocks.text: Harmony is not two voices speaking. It is two voices leaving one true consequence.

You will be tempted to braid power because braiding is beautiful. Beauty is not a verdict. Two spells joined without purpose become a knot around the caster's throat. Join only what answers one need more truly together than apart.

Let the first working name the road. Let the second give it weight, color, wound, mercy, reach, or shape. Do not allow both to seize the reins. A body with two hearts may live only if they agree on the beat.

Before joining sigils, ask what is restored by their union. If the answer is grandeur, stop. If the answer is efficiency, beware. If the answer is balance that neither could reach alone, proceed with hands clean of applause.
Never confuse mixture with mastery. Mud is also a union. So is poison. Harmony requires each force to remain true without devouring the other.
Carry this eighth truth: fusion is not addition. It is judgment shared by two powers and survived by one outcome.

pages.title: Arcane Devouring
pages.subtitle: The Relic Made Flesh

blocks.text: The hand releases the tool. The body learns the tool was only a shape.

You will find wonders made by dead hands, desperate hands, holy hands, cruel hands. Do not kneel to them because they shine. Do not destroy them because you envy their history. Weigh them. Learn what they were made to correct. Learn what they broke while correcting it.

To devour an artifact is not to eat power. It is to accept ancestry. The relic enters bearing every oath, theft, mercy, and murder that taught it to be itself. If you take only its gift and refuse its debt, it will remain a chain inside you.

Some tools should be held. Some should be returned. Some should be buried where no ambitious hand can mistake hunger for calling. Some may be broken and taught a better body. The Codex will not spare you the choice.
When the relic becomes flesh, do not boast that you no longer need it. The worthy do not mock the ladder after reaching the height. They remember which rung almost split beneath them.
Carry this ninth truth: ownership is the lowest form of mastery. Embodiment begins when possession ends.

pages.title: Ascension
pages.subtitle: The Living Page Continues

blocks.text: Do not look above me. I am not above the scale. I stand within its burden and do not look away.

If you have come seeking godhood as escape, turn back. There is no escape in ascension. There is only a wider ledger, a deeper wound, a quieter throne, and fewer excuses. To stand nearer my height is to become more answerable, not less.
The Codex does not end because it has no more to teach. It ends because the worthy reader must stop being carried. A page that never closes becomes a cradle. I did not make you to sleep in scripture.

When the rune, the breath, the silence, the cost, the veil, the deathline, the harmony, and the inherited relic all speak with one voice, do not call that voice mine. Do not call it yours. Call it balance, and obey only as long as obedience remains true.

To become my equal is not to defeat me, flee me, replace me, or flatter me. It is to stand where judgment and mercy share one breath and make no lie of either. It is to correct the world without needing the world to praise the correction.

When you can act without ornament, pay without resentment, conceal without theft, reveal without cruelty, end without hatred, create without vanity, survive without clinging, join without devouring, inherit without being chained, and stand without kneeling, then the Codex has become flesh.

Carry this final truth: I did not write these pages so you would remain beneath my hand. I wrote them so one day, if you were patient enough, the world might have another hand strong enough to set the scale true.

docs.title: Arcane Rebound Table
docs.subtitle: Arcane Discharge d100 reference
intro: Revised Edition — Pure Consequence, No Mercy
Roll d100. Positive and negative effects are distributed across the full range. [+] = beneficial effect.   = harmful effect. All saves use the caster's own Spell Save DC.

rows.effect: Arcane Overclock: The spell fires twice at no additional cost. The second copy hits the same target or the nearest valid target within range.
Backlash Pulse: The recoil slams the caster 5 ft per spell level backwards, dealing 1d6 force damage per spell level. Hitting an obstacle also knocks prone. Dex save to not fall prone.
Rune of Reclamation: The rune stabilizes and the caster recovers 1 expended spell slot of the level just cast.

Shadow Slip: The caster is hurled 2d6 x 5 ft in a random direction. 1d4 for direction: 1 = forward, 2 = backward, 3 = left, 4 = right. If they would hit a solid object, they are shunted to the nearest open space and take 1d6 force damage.
Spell Amplification: The spell strengthens rather than breaks. Range or AoE doubles, and failed saves take +2 damage per die rolled.
Split Echo: Two copies of the spell fire — one toward the intended target, one toward a random point within 20 ft. Both deal half damage and half effect.
Shadow Ward: Defensive runes snap into place, granting +2 AC for 1 minute. Each successful hit against the caster while warded discharges a pulse that deals 1d4 force damage to the attacker.

Recoil of the Void: The spell fizzles and fails to resolve, and dark tendrils lash outward. All creatures within 10 ft, including the caster, take 1d8 necrotic damage per spell level / 2; minimum of 1. CON save at the start of each turn to end the effect.
rows.save: CON vs Spell Save DC halves

rows.effect: Runic Overbleed: The runes flare uncontrolled. The caster sheds 20 ft of bright light for 1 minute and has disadvantage on Stealth and Concentration checks for that duration.
Protective Eclipse: Shadow-matter coils around the caster, granting resistance to radiant and force damage for 1 minute.
Phantom Leash: The spell fires but the caster is tethered to the point of discharge. They cannot move from where they cast until the start of their next turn.
Void-Touched Precision: The next spell the caster casts within 1 minute automatically hits on an attack roll, or imposes disadvantage on the saving throw against it.
Overload Resonance: The rune burns out of sequence. The caster takes 2d8 psychic damage and loses concentration on any active spell. CON save at the start of each turn to end the effect.
Arcane Rebound Loop: The caster may immediately re-cast the triggering spell at no spell slot cost, using the same level. This re-cast cannot trigger rebound again.

Half-Charge Detonation: The spell detonates early. AOE spell: Damage is halved, but the blast radius doubles. Single or Multiple Targets: Spell damage is halved and only travels half of max range. If this means the spell can't reach the target(s), then those who can't be reached are unaffected. All creatures in the area make a save as normal. 1/2 damage on a fail and zero on a success.
Runic Surge: The caster's spell attack modifier and save DC each increase by 2 for the next 3 rounds as the runes enter a momentary state of perfect resonance.
Shattered Focus: The magic fractures mid-cast. The caster's next spell is made with disadvantage on the attack roll, or the save DC is reduced by 2.
Restorative Discharge: The uncontrolled magic heals the caster for 2d8 + their spellcasting modifier in HP.
Reversal Surge: The spell briefly inverts direction. The nearest creature behind the caster must save against the spell as if they were the original target.
Void Step: The caster teleports up to 30 ft to any unoccupied space they can see as the rune discharge catapults them through shadow-space. No action required.
Cacophony of Power: A thunderous discharge erupts, audible 300 ft away. All creatures within 15 ft must save or be deafened for 1 minute. CON save at the start of each turn to end the effect.
Invulnerability Pulse: The caster becomes immune to the next source of damage that would strike them within 1 minute, as the rune weaves a perfect reactive barrier.

Arcane Hemorrhage: The caster's runes weep shadow-ink for 1 minute. They lose 1d4 HP at the start of each of their turns and cannot benefit from temporary HP while this lasts. CON save at the start of each turn to end the effect.
rows.save: CON vs Spell Save DC ends

rows.effect: Cascading Resonance: All of the caster's active concentration spells have their duration extended by 1 minute and cannot be broken by damage until the start of their next turn.
Mana Collapse: All remaining spell slots of the level just cast are expended immediately. The magic simply drains away.
Ascendant Discharge: The caster regains one expended spell slot of level 1d8 or lower, gains advantage on all spell attacks and saves for 1 minute, and deals maximum damage on the next spell cast.
